EXCLUSIVEAnn Widdecombe suspect is a 'loner' who 'drove nearly 300 miles to ex-MP's home with a foot-long pole' on day she was 'murdered': Police search house in Rotherham

The suspect being quizzed over Ann Widdecombe's murder is a 28-year-old 'loner' who is believed to have driven 275 miles to her Devon home armed with a 'foot-long pole'. The man, who the Mail is not naming, was arrested on suspicion of killing the former Tory MP after a dozen armed officers descended on his council house in South Yorkshire on Saturday night. He is understood to have been captured on CCTV leaving the property on a rundown Rotherham estate between 6.30am and 7am on Wednesday - the morning of the 78-year-old's death.
  • 1 video

How in the world do you write an entire article without putting the name of the pub? Good grief. RIP to all those who lost their lives.

At least 27 people have died after a huge fire engulfed a pub in Bangkok early on Monday morning, officials said. Rescuers said the fire was reported around midnight. Footage shared online by first responders shows huge blaze raging out of the front door of the pub in the northern part of the Thai capital as people tried to flee, with thick black smoke pluming into the sky. Thailand Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul told reporters at the scene that 27 people died and several have been taken to the hospital. He said the cause of the fire is still under investigation.

Why are people having a go at Tuchel because he said their performance wasn't good enough against Norway. It really wasn't. The end result was what was required but the manner in which we ground out that result wasn't. It was less than good and at best it was average. Kane spend most of the game on his backside trying to con the ref for free kicks and penalties instead of getting on with the game. The ref was having none of it but that message wasn't getting through. He should have been subbed much earlier to buck his ideas up. Norway was the better side and had a perfectly good goal disallowed. Haaland had every right to push back and if you fall over that's your fault. Get back up. The ball isn't in play until the corner has been kicked.

I went on Androfemme for 3 months. My libido improved within a month, my mojo and zest for life also improved and finally the brain fog lifted which was amazing. However after about 6-8 weeks I started to get a racing heart and anxiety so I had to stop. I did ask GP if i could just use it once a week as I’d already reduced it to every 2 or 3 days, she said yes but in the meantime work on increasing oestrogen as that was still quite low. I’ve done that but been afraid to re-start it. I was quite low for a few month’s once I stopped taking it as I could see all the benefits it had provided but a racing heart was quite scary.

Jannik Sinner was greeted by the Prince and Princess of Wales and two of their children after winning the Wimbledon men's singles final tonight. The champion shared a laugh with Prince George, 12, Princess Charlotte, 11, and their parents shortly after Kate presented him with the trophy for his win against Germany's Alexander Zverev. Meanwhile their younger brother Prince Louis, eight, was notably absent from the group as he stayed at home.

As a procession of luxury cars swept through the gates of the King's estate in Gloucestershire on Friday - carrying guests for an exclusive Chanel luncheon - one vehicle attracted little attention. Inside were Prince Harry, his wife Meghan and their children Archie, seven, and Lilibet, five, who had arrived at Highgrove for a private family meeting. Far from the cameras and public gaze, the Sussexes entered Charles's 900-acre site unnoticed as dozens of guests turned up for the French fashion house's event to honour students who had completed an elite 24-week embroidery course.

From the North Pole to the summit of Mount Everest, it might seem like intrepid explorers have already conquered every remaining frontier. However, you might be surprised to learn just how many places on Earth have never been visited by a single living human. From the impenetrable heart of ancient forests to the frozen peaks of sacred mountains, these are the last few truly untouched locations. And, in the hidden depths of caves, there could be hundreds of miles of pristine territory that are still waiting to be discovered.

Ukraine attacks military targets whilst Putin slaughters innocent women and children.

This is the terrifying moment Ukrainians were forced to hurl themselves to the ground as a Russian strike blew out the windows of a coffee shop.  CCTV footage from the Piccolino coffee shop in Sumy showed men, women and children leaping to safety as a thunderous explosion was heard.  People were seen enjoying their day, with many walking around outside and a few people sat on beanbags soaking up the sun.

as humans, we should be ashamed and disgusted to allow this happen all for the purpose of cheap entertainment. please keep these beautiful animals free and safe. teach young people to protect them!

When Julie took her first steps of freedom into her new home at an elephant sanctuary her 40 years living and working in a circus were finally at an end. Taken from her mother who was killed for her ivory tusks when she was just a calf in the 1980s, Julie was shipped out of southern Africa and sold into the circus trade. She was bought by the family-run Victor Hugo Cardinali Circus which is the largest in Portugal.
  • 7 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Portugal's last circus elephant finds forever home in sanctuary
